relabelled_senses.definition: May it be far from me; originally a literal wish that a thing be kept distant from the speaker.

A pattern of suntan in which areas of skin exposed by typical outdoor clothing—especially forearms and lower legs—are noticeably darker than areas covered by sleeves or garments, resulting from prolonged outdoor work or activity.
A now-offensive historical term used to refer to a Black slave or labourer employed in manual farm work.
The goalpost or area of the goal that is furthest from the ball or the player making a cross; the side of the goal opposite the near post.
A colloquial, jocular term for a ceiling fan or extractor fan installed in a bathroom.
Arriving deliberately after the scheduled or expected time for a social event in order to convey style, status, or nonchalance.
To trouble oneself, to worry, or to take pains about something; to be concerned or bothered.
Happening with great speed, energy, and intensity; rapid and vigorous in action or development.
Sleeping deeply and soundly, not easily awakened.
An agreement or pact in which a person compromises or sacrifices moral, ethical, or spiritual values in exchange for power, wealth, knowledge, or other immediate benefits.
A cisgender woman who performs as a drag queen, adopting the clothing, makeup, and performance style associated with drag culture.
A bird; any creature that has feathers.
To insert coins or otherwise pay into a parking meter (or similar timed payment device) to extend the permitted parking time.
To be at liberty or to feel able to do something without hesitation or fear of causing offense; often used with a to-infinitive to grant informal permission.
A problem concerning the correct handling of boundary or off‑by‑one conditions when counting, indexing, or iterating over discrete elements.
To make a solemn admonition or address to those about to receive communion, intended to deter the unworthy from approaching the Lord's Supper.
To engage in argument or verbal disputation; to defend a position and attempt to demonstrate its truth through argument.
To make a circuit or sail a roundabout course; to go around an obstacle by a curved route.
To go and retrieve items and bring them to someone; to run errands involving fetching and carrying.
